signals.reasoning: Even with the event board cleared, the live SOL short is still a sizable loser after fully round-tripping peak PnL, retains none of that peak edge, still has no stop, and liquidation distance remains under 1%. This is still a cleanup exit, not a healthy thesis hold.

BTC has stabilized and reclaimed enough ground that pressing fresh bearish exposure no longer looks attractive, but it is also not a better immediate decision than simply flattening the failed SOL short.
ETH is participating in the same recovery tape and does not present a cleaner next step than getting out of the failed SOL position.
MERL has constructive recovery structure, but rotating into another rebound name is lower quality than first cleaning up the open SOL risk.
ORDI is rebounding strongly, but it is already extended enough that it does not offer a disciplined fresh call right after a failed short.
TST remains far too extended and reactive to trust as a fresh decision.

STABLE still has strong squeeze fuel and is one of the more constructive names on the board, but the immediate priority in position management is still flattening the failed SOL short rather than rotating into a new long.
FARTCOIN is mixed now that the fresh-shorts-pressing edge has softened into a more neutral recovery tape.
WCT has rebound momentum, but the setup is already too stretched and messy to justify treating it as a better immediate action than simply getting flat.
ZEREBRO still looks more like noisy short-covering than a clean fresh trade, so it is not where disciplined attention should go right now.
